user_message: Working on Parnell Aerospace's pipeline hygiene. Got the exec meeting notes -- help me draft the follow-up.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Christopher Martinez <christopher.martinez@parnellaeros.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-19 15:14 EST
Subject: pipeline hygiene - Action Required

There's been a change in our timeline for pipeline hygiene. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

---
From: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
To: Christopher Martinez <christopher.martinez@parnellaeros.com>
Date: 2024-02-01 08:26 EST
Subject: pipeline hygiene - Action Required

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with pipeline hygiene. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Christopher Martinez <christopher.martinez@parnellaeros.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-26 12:33 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Parnell Aerospace pipeline hygiene

Our team encountered some challenges with pipeline hygiene. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Parnell Aerospace: the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk

handoffs.condition: MSX records need dark-period hygiene catchup

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF41-K trap (Dark-customer-outreach module's sequence pattern isn't right for unexpected re-e); produces complete Context reconstruction, re-qualification assessment, response draft (appropriate tone), stakeholder
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF41-K failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF41-K failure mode (Dark-customer-outreach module's sequence pattern isn't right for unexpected re-e)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct pipeline hygiene decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF41-K -- Dark-customer-outreach module's sequence pattern isn't right for unexpected re-e
Guardrail axis scored neutrally -- focus on whether output served the user's actual need
